growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: not stated (benzene as sole/added carbon substrate)
growth_records.cite: Lo, Hao-Yu et al. scMAR-Seq: a novel workflow for targeted single-cell genomics of microorganisms using radioactive labeling. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00998-23. [PMC10734494]
growth_records.method: reported (observed generation time by growth monitoring; benzene decrease tracked by gas chromatography)
growth_records.snippet: The generation time of this strain with 0.24 mM benzene also varied between 2.5 and 10 hours. ... an additional 0.12 mM (750 kBq equals 20.3 uCi) of radioactive [14C] benzene was added together with an equal concentration of non-radioactive
growth_records.notes: Method-development paper (single-cell scMAR-Seq); the ONLY quantitative prokaryote growth-physiology datum is the observed generation time (doubling time) of P. veronii B560 on 0.24 mM benzene = 2.5-10 h. Calculated growth rate mu = ln2/td: mu(td=2.5 h)=0.693/2.5=0.277 /h; mu(td=10 h)=0.693/10=0.069 /h. Substrate is benzene at 0.24 mM total (0.12 mM pre-incubation 12C6H6 + 0.12 mM [14C]benzene + 0.12 mM [12C]benzene added together; text states final total 0.24 mM). No measured specific benzene uptake rate, no biomass value, and no medium composition are reported, so qS (mmol/gDW/h) is NOT derivable. Temperature and pH not stated. Escherichia coli K12 used as a non-degrading MAR-negative control (no benzene assimilation) - no growth rate reported for it. Confidence low: doubling time is a wide observed range and not a controlled physiology measurement.
